title: Rogue Dungeon: A Rogue's Tale - all about the new expansion
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R1S8PFUsVWg

Daniel (@dungeondive) sits down with John, Albert, and Chad—the creators of Rogue Dungeon—to discuss "A Rogue's Tale," the boldest expansion yet. Instead of just adding cards, they're introducing a full campaign layer: hex crawling that connects bite-sized dungeons, a moon phase system that tracks passage of time, and random events drawn from terrain-appropriate decks that progress narrative alongside combat. Rather than rolling dice for random encounters, players spend coins to "chart their course," paying for provisions while coins themselves serve dual purposes—one side shows travel, the other reveals whether an encounter occurs. Brilliant economy of design.

What makes A Rogue's Tale narratively ambitious is its embrace of moral consequence and emergent storytelling. Your character can accumulate marks—become a murderer, flee the law, do hard time in prison—and these stick with you. Towns offer genuine services (training, stables, temples of justice). The four environmental packs—dungeon, tomb, sewer, castle—work standalone or integrated into campaigns. The castle especially shines with choose-your-own-adventure structure; you navigate six distinct castle sections choosing which five to explore. Campaign completion spans 10-20 sessions with steady character progression. Custom card holders designed from community feedback ensure even this sprawling expansion fits a 3x4 table. After years of asking for expansion content, Rogue Dungeon fans are finally getting exactly what they wanted.

----

Which aspect of A Rogue's Tale excites you most: the hex crawl layer, the narrative consequences, or the modular environmental packs?
